1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report for the user:

## Monorepo Package Creation Conventions - Complete Report

Based on my exploration of the Prisma monorepo, here's the comprehensive guide for creating a new package:

### 1. **Minimal package.json Template**

For a standard library package (unbundled):
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/my-package",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/my-package"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

For an adapter package (bundled with multiple entry points):
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/adapter-name",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma's driver adapter for ...",
  "main": "dist/index-node.js",
  "module": "dist/index-node.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index-node.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index-node.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index-node.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index-node.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index-node.mjs"
      }
    },
    "./web": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index-web.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index-web.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index-web.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index-web.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/my-package"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist",
    "README.md"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false,
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/other-package": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

Key notes:
- Use `"workspace:*"` for internal dependencies
- Always include `"sideEffects": false` for tree-shaking
- Both `.js` (CJS) and `.mjs` (ESM) outputs are generated
- The `.d.mts` file is used for ESM type definitions (note the different extension in credentials-store)

---

### 2. **tsconfig.json and tsconfig.build.json Patterns**

**Package tsconfig.json** (includes all files including tests):
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**Package tsconfig.build.json** (for compilation, excludes tests):
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Optional: If you need a custom rootDir:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

**Root tsconfig.build.regular.json** defines:
- Target: `ES2022`
- Module: `commonjs`
- Library: `ES2022`, `DOM`
- ESModuleInterop, isolatedModules, sourceMap, declaration, strict mode
- Excludes tests, dist, node_modules, sandbox, fixtures

---

### 3. **helpers/build.ts Pattern**

For **unbundled packages** (multiple output files):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { unbundledConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(unbundledConfig)
```

For **bundled adapter packages** (single bundle):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

For **custom multi-entry bundled packages**:
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

const defaultBuildOptions = adapterConfig.map((opts) => ({
  ...opts,
  entryPoints: ['src/index-node.ts'],
  outfile: 'dist/index-node',
}))

const webBuildOptions = adapterConfig.map((opts) => ({
  ...opts,
  entryPoints: ['src/index-web.ts'],
  outfile: 'dist/index-web',
}))

void build([...defaultBuildOptions, ...webBuildOptions])
```

For **custom single ESM build with plugins**:
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { esmSplitCodeToCjs } from '../../../helpers/compile/plugins/esmSplitCodeToCjs'

void build([
  {
    name: 'default',
    bundle: true,
    emitTypes: true,
    splitting: true,
    format: 'esm',
    plugins: [esmSplitCodeToCjs],
    external: ['@prisma/debug', '@prisma/dmmf', '@prisma/generator'],
  },
])
```

**Build config types** (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`):
- `unbundledConfig`: Multiple output files in `dist/`, CJS and ESM, emits types
- `adapterConfig`: Single bundled entry (`src/index.ts` → `dist/index`), CJS and ESM, emits types
- Custom `BuildOptions` extend esbuild with `name`, `emitTypes`, `emitMetafile`
- Automatically handles: resolveExtensions, mainFields, external dependencies, plugins

---

### 4. **Root tsconfig.build.bundle.json - Paths Section**

Located at `/tsconfig.build.bundle.json`, defines path aliases for all packages:

```json
{
  "extends": "./tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "module": "preserve",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "paths": {
      "@prisma/client": ["./packages/client/src"],
      "@prisma/client-common": ["./packages/client-common/src"],
      "@prisma/dmmf": ["./packages/dmmf/src"],
      "@prisma/generator": ["./packages/generator/src"],
      "@prisma/debug": ["./packages/debug/src"],
      "@prisma/adapter-libsql": ["./packages/adapter-libsql/src"],
      // ... etc
    }
  }
}
```

**To add a new package:**
Add entry: `"@prisma/my-package": ["./packages/my-package/src"]`

---

### 5. **CI Test Registration - .github/workflows/test-template.yml**

For new packages, add them to the **"others" job** (lines 800-879), which tests miscellaneous packages:

```yaml
  others:
    name: Others [v${{ matrix.node }}]
    timeout-minutes: 10
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    if: contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-')
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest]
        node: ['20.19.0', '22.12.0', '24']
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install & build
        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
          skip-tsc: true

      - run: pnpm run test
        name: 'my-package'
        working-directory: packages/my-package
```

**If your package has special requirements:**
- Create a dedicated job (like `internals`, `migrate`, `cli-commands`)
- Specify matrix strategies (Node versions, Docker services, etc.)
- Use conditional `if:` statements to filter when it runs

**Current package locations in CI:**
- **client-packages** job: client-common, client-engine-runtime, ts-builders, client-generator-*
- **others** job: debug, generator-helper, get-platform, fetch-engine, engines, instrumentation, schema-files-loader, config, dmmf, generator, credentials-store, sqlcommenter-*
- **driver-adapter-unit-tests** job: driver adapters (libsql, mariadb, d1, pg, planetscale, etc.)
- **Special jobs**: client, migrate, cli, internals, integration-tests

---

### 6. **Workspace Setup - pnpm-workspace.yaml and Root package.json**

**pnpm-workspace.yaml:**
```yaml
onlyBuiltDependencies:
  - '@swc/core'
  - better-sqlite3
  - esbuild
  - sharp
  - sqlite3
  - workerd
  - yarn

packages:
  - 'packages/*'

peerDependencyRules:
  allowedVersions:
    '@octokit/core': '>=3'
```

The `packages: ['packages/*']` glob automatically includes all new packages in `packages/` directory.

**Root package.json:**
- Build orchestration via Turbo: `"build": "turbo build"`
- Node requirement: `^20.19 || ^22.12 || >=24.0`
- pnpm requirement: `>=10.15 <11`

---

### 7. **Minimal File Structure for New Package**

```
packages/my-package/
├── package.json                 (from template above)
├── tsconfig.json               (extends ../../tsconfig.json)
├── tsconfig.build.json         (extends ../../tsconfig.build.regular.json)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts               (build configuration)
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts               (main entry point)
│   └── [other source files]
├── tests/                     (optional)
│   └── index.test.ts
└── README.md
```

---

### Key Convention Highlights:

1. **Naming**: All public packages use `@prisma/` scope
2. **Build system**: esbuild via custom helpers, generates both CJS and ESM
3. **TypeScript**: Strict mode, single outDir, declaration files included
4. **Dependencies**: Use `workspace:*` for monorepo references
5. **Exports**: Dual CJS/ESM with proper type exports for both
6. **CI**: Automatically runs tests if added to test-template.yml with `pnpm run test`
7. **Version**: All packages start at `0.0.0` (managed by publish system)

## Prisma Generator Packages Structure Report

Based on my exploration of this monorepo, here's the comprehensive structure and implementation details of the Prisma generator packages:

---

### **1. Package.json Structure**

All three generator packages share a common pattern:

**Common Fields:**
- **name**: `@prisma/client-generator-js`, `@prisma/client-generator-ts`, `@prisma/client-generator-registry`
- **version**: `0.0.0`
- **description**: "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use"
- **main**: `dist/index.js`
- **module**: `dist/index.mjs`
- **types**: `dist/index.d.ts`
- **license**: `Apache-2.0`

**Exports (ESM/CJS dual format):**
```json
{
  ".": {
    "require": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/index.js"
    },
    "import": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.mts" (or "./dist/index.d.ts" for registry),
      "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
    }
  }
}
```

**Build Scripts:**
- `dev`: `DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts` (watch mode)
- `build`: `tsx helpers/build.ts` (compile)
- `prepublishOnly`: `pnpm run build`
- `test`: `vitest run [--testTimeout 30000]`

**Files** published: `["dist"]`
**SideEffects**: `false`

**Dependencies** vary slightly:
- Both JS and TS generators depend on: `@prisma/client-common`, `@prisma/debug`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/engines-version`, `@prisma/fetch-engine`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/get-platform`, `@prisma/internals`, `@prisma/param-graph-builder`, `@prisma/ts-builders`, plus utilities like `pluralize`, `klona`, `ts-pattern`
- TS generator additionally has: `fast-glob`, `get-tsconfig`
- Registry depends on: JS generator, TS generator, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals`

---

### **2. TypeScript Configuration**

**tsconfig.json (both generators):**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**tsconfig.build.json (both generators):**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

2. **Write phase**: `writeFileMap(outputDir, fileMap)` recursively writes files:
   ```typescript
   function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {
     return Promise.all(
       Object.entries(fileMap).map(async ([fileName, content]) => {
         const absolutePath = path.join(outputDir, fileName)
         await fs.rm(absolutePath, { recursive: true, force: true })  // Delete first
         if (typeof content === 'string' || Buffer.isBuffer(content)) {
           await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)  // Write file
         } else {

         }
       })

   }
   ```

- `client.js` / `client.d.ts` - Named client export

- `index-browser.js` - Browser variant
- `package.json` - Generated exports map
- `schema.prisma` - Copy of data model
- `*.wasm` files - Query compiler WASM artifacts
- Nested directories with additional type definitions and utilities

**Special handling:**

- Supports both string and Buffer content (TS generator handles WASM buffers)
- Preambles added to files (e.g., `@ts-nocheck` for TS generator)
- Copies runtime source maps if configured

```
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── tsconfig.json
│   ├── tsconfig.build.json
```

1. **Interface contract** (`@prisma/generator` → `Generator` type)
2. **Implementation classes** (JS, TS) that implement `getManifest()` and `generate()`

## Comprehensive Report: Prisma Generator Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the generator system across both the `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/generator` packages. Here's the complete API surface and structure:

### 1. **generatorHandler Function API**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

```typescript
export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

The `Handler` interface defines two methods:

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}
```

**Protocol Details**:
- Uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout
- Listens on stdin for JSON-encoded messages with `method` field
- Responds via stderr with JSON-RPC response format
- Two methods are expected: `generate` and `getManifest`
- Errors are caught and returned as JSON-RPC error responses with code `-32000`

---

### 2. **GeneratorManifest Type**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}

export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
```

**Optional manifest returned by onManifest callback**. Describes generator capabilities and constraints.

---

### 3. **GeneratorOptions Type**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
  generator: GeneratorConfig

  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string // version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

The main input to `onGenerate()`. Contains the DMMF document and schema information.

---

### 4. **GeneratorConfig Type**

```typescript
export interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: {
    output?: never
    provider?: never
    binaryTargets?: never
    previewFeatures?: never
  } & {
    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
  }
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export interface EnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: null | string
  value: null | string
}

export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: string | null
  value: string
  native?: boolean
}
```

Config passed to both `onManifest` and in `GeneratorOptions.generator`. The `config` field holds custom generator options (anything not named `output`, `provider`, `binaryTargets`, or `previewFeatures`).

---

### 5. **DataSource Type**

```typescript
export interface DataSource {
  name: string
  provider: ConnectorType
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  schemas: string[] | []
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export type ConnectorType =
  | 'mysql'
  | 'mongodb'
  | 'sqlite'
  | 'postgresql'
  | 'postgres'
  | 'prisma+postgres'
  | 'sqlserver'
  | 'cockroachdb'

export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
```

### 6. **DMMF Document Structure**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
}
```

#### **Datamodel** (schema models/types)
```typescript
export type Datamodel = {
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}

export type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}

export type Field = {
  kind: FieldKind  // 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string  // BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, etc., or model name
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]

}

export type FieldDefault = {

}

export type Index = {
  model: string
  type: IndexType  // 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

}
```

#### **Schema** (GraphQL-like schema for Query/Mutation generation)

```typescript
export type Schema = {

  }

    grouping?: string

export type OutputType = {

export type SchemaArg = {
  name: string

  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean

export type SchemaField = {

}
```

#### **Mappings** (Prisma client operation names)
```typescript
export type Mappings = {
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}

export type ModelMapping = {
  model: string

  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null

  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
```

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive Report: Prisma Generator Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the generator system across both the `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/generator` packages. Here's the complete API surface and structure:

### 1. **generatorHandler Function API**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

```typescript
export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

The `Handler` interface defines two methods:

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}
```

**Protocol Details**:
- Uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout
- Listens on stdin for JSON-encoded messages with `method` field
- Responds via stderr with JSON-RPC response format
- Two methods are expected: `generate` and `getManifest`
- Errors are caught and returned as JSON-RPC error responses with code `-32000`

---

### 2. **GeneratorManifest Type**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}

export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
```

**Optional manifest returned by onManifest callback**. Describes generator capabilities and constraints.

---

### 3. **GeneratorOptions Type**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string // version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

The main input to `onGenerate()`. Contains the DMMF document and schema information.

---

### 4. **GeneratorConfig Type**

```typescript
export interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: {
    output?: never
    provider?: never
    binaryTargets?: never
    previewFeatures?: never
  } & {
    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
  }
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export interface EnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: null | string
  value: null | string
}

export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: string | null
  value: string
  native?: boolean
}
```

Config passed to both `onManifest` and in `GeneratorOptions.generator`. The `config` field holds custom generator options (anything not named `output`, `provider`, `binaryTargets`, or `previewFeatures`).

---

### 5. **DataSource Type**

```typescript
export interface DataSource {
  name: string
  provider: ConnectorType
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  schemas: string[] | []
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export type ConnectorType =
  | 'mysql'
  | 'mongodb'
  | 'sqlite'
  | 'postgresql'
  | 'postgres'
  | 'prisma+postgres'
  | 'sqlserver'
  | 'cockroachdb'

export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
```

---

### 6. **DMMF Document Structure**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775389418750-ejvz3i/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Document = {
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}
```

#### **Datamodel** (schema models/types)
```typescript
export type Datamodel = {
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}

export type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}

export type Field = {
  kind: FieldKind  // 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string  // BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, etc., or model name
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldDefault = {
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}

export type Index = {
  model: string
  type: IndexType  // 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}

export type DatamodelEnum = {
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}

export type EnumValue = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}
```

#### **Schema** (GraphQL-like schema for Query/Mutation generation)
```typescript
export type Schema = {
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}

export type InputType = {
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}

export type OutputType = {
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}

export type SchemaArg = {
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}

export type SchemaField = {
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}
```

#### **Mappings** (Prisma client operation names)
```typescript
export type Mappings = {
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}

export type ModelMapping = {
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}
```

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

export { generatorHandler } from './generatorHandler'
export { GeneratorError, GeneratorProcess } from './GeneratorProcess'

/**
 * Exported for backwards compatibility only.
 *
 * @deprecated Generators using `@prisma/generator-helper` shouldn't need JSON-RPC internals.
 */
export type * as JsonRPC from './json-rpc'

/**
 * A re-export for backwards compatibility with community generators.
 *
 * @deprecated Use the `@prisma/dmmf` package instead.
 */
export type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

/**
 * A re-export for backwards compatibility with community generators.
 *
 * @deprecated Use the `@prisma/generator` package instead.
 */
export type * from '@prisma/generator'

Design an implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package `@prisma/generator-rest-client` at `packages/generator-rest-client/`.

## Context

This is the Prisma ORM monorepo. We need to create a new generator that produces a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema's DMMF. The generator should follow existing monorepo patterns exactly.

## How Prisma Generators Work

1. **Generator protocol**: A generator is a Node process that communicates via JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr. The `generatorHandler()` function from `@prisma/generator-helper` handles the protocol. It accepts a `Handler` with:
   - `onManifest(config: GeneratorConfig)` → returns `GeneratorManifest` (prettyName, defaultOutput, etc.)
   - `onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions)` → receives DMMF document and writes output files

2. **Key types** (from `@prisma/generator`):
   - `GeneratorOptions.dmmf` is a `DMMF.Document` containing `datamodel.models`, `datamodel.enums`, `schema`, `mappings`
   - `DMMF.Model` has: `name`, `fields: Field[]`, `primaryKey`, `uniqueFields`, etc.
   - `DMMF.Field` has: `kind` ('scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported'), `name`, `type` (string - e.g. 'String', 'Int', 'Boolean', 'DateTime', 'ModelName'), `isRequired`, `isList`, `isId`, `isUnique`, `hasDefaultValue`, `relationFromFields?`, `relationName?`
   - `GeneratorOptions.generator.output.value` is the output directory path
   - `GeneratorOptions.generator.config` holds custom key-value config from the schema

3. **External generators** (like this one) use a `bin` entry in `package.json` pointing to a script that calls `generatorHandler()`. The `provider` in the Prisma schema matches the bin name. Usage:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   ```

## Monorepo Package Structure Conventions

Based on existing packages like `@prisma/credentials-store`:

**package.json pattern:**
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "bin": { "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/bin.js" },
  "exports": { ".": { "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" }, "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" } } },
  "scripts": { "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts", "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts", "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build", "test": "vitest run" },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false,
  "dependencies": { "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*", "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*", "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*" }
}
```

**tsconfig.json**: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }`
**tsconfig.build.json**: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }`
**helpers/build.ts**: Imports `build` from `../../../helpers/compile/build` and `adapterConfig` from `../../../helpers/compile/configs`, calls `void build(adapterConfig)`.

**Root tsconfig.build.bundle.json** needs a new path entry: `"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]`

## What the Generator Should Produce

Given a schema with models, the generator writes a single `rest-client.ts` file to the output directory. The file should contain:
1. A typed TypeScript interface for each model (only scalar/enum fields, skip relations)
2. A main `RestClient` class that accepts a `baseUrl` in its constructor
3. For each model, the class exposes CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
4. Methods are typed with the model's interface
5. Enum types as TypeScript string union types

## Requirements

- `src/index.ts`: Entry point that re-exports the generator handler and can be imported as a library
- `src/bin.ts`: Bin script (`#!/usr/bin/env node`) that calls `generatorHandler` with the handler
- `src/generator.ts`: The `onGenerate` implementation - takes GeneratorOptions, extracts DMMF, calls template functions, writes `rest-client.ts` to output dir using `fs.promises`
- `src/template.ts`: Pure string-template functions for code generation (type mapping, interface generation, method generation, etc.)
- `src/__tests__/generator.test.ts`: Unit tests for the generation logic using vitest
- Follow kebab-case file naming
- Use `node:fs/promises` and `node:path` for file I/O
- Map Prisma types to TS types: String→string, Int→number, Float→number, Boolean→boolean, DateTime→string, BigInt→bigint, Decimal→string, Json→unknown, Bytes→string, etc.
- Enums from DMMF should become TypeScript union types

Please design a detailed step-by-step plan with file contents sketches and all files that need to be created or modified.

32	}>

37	}>

53	}>

55	  name: string
56	  dbName: string | null
57	  schema: string | null
58	  fields: Field[]

5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

6	import { Credentials, CredentialsStore } from '../src'
